grainFreeDryFood.name: Canagan Free-Run Chicken
grainFreeDryFood.mainIngredient: Freshly prepared deboned chicken (26%), dried chicken (25%)
grainFreeDryFood.summary: Premium British-made grain-free kibble with 60% chicken content. Includes joint-supporting glucosamine and MSM, plus sweet potato for slow-release energy. One of the most popular grain-free options in the UK.
pros: 60% chicken content — genuinely high meat
UK-made with traceable ingredients
Includes glucosamine + MSM for joints
Sweet potato instead of white potato
cons: Premium price — £8.33/kg
Only one protein source (chicken)
Some dogs dislike the small kibble size

grainFreeDryFood.name: Orijen Original
grainFreeDryFood.mainIngredient: Fresh chicken meat (13%), fresh turkey meat (7%), fresh whole eggs (7%)
grainFreeDryFood.summary: The gold standard of grain-free dog food. 85% quality animal ingredients with multiple protein sources mimicking a natural diet. WholePrey ratios include organs and cartilage for complete nutrition without synthetic supplements.
pros: 85% animal ingredients — highest on this list
Multiple protein sources (chicken, turkey, fish)
WholePrey ratios include organs for natural nutrition
No synthetic amino acid supplements needed
cons: Very expensive — £13.33/kg
Rich formula may cause loose stools initially
Strong fish smell some owners dislike

grainFreeDryFood.name: Lily's Kitchen Chicken & Duck
grainFreeDryFood.mainIngredient: Freshly prepared chicken (25%), freshly prepared duck (10%)
grainFreeDryFood.summary: Well-known premium British brand offering natural grain-free recipes with 50% freshly prepared meat. Certified B Corp with ethical sourcing. Popular choice with good availability in supermarkets and pet shops.
pros: Widely available in UK supermarkets
B Corp certified — ethical production
No artificial preservatives, fillers, or derivatives
Good range of flavours and sizes
cons: Lower meat content than competitors (50%)
Contains potato starch as filler
Premium price for the meat percentage

grainFreeDryFood.name: Symply Fresh Turkey
grainFreeDryFood.summary: Outstanding value grain-free option from a well-regarded UK brand. Uses freshly prepared turkey as the sole protein — ideal for dogs with chicken sensitivities. Sweet potato and pea-based carbohydrates.
pros: Excellent value at £6.33/kg
Single protein source — great for allergies
UK-made with high-quality turkey
No chicken or common allergens
cons: Lower protein than premium brands
Limited flavour range
Less well-known brand

grainFreeDryFood.name: Eden Holistic Original Cuisine
grainFreeDryFood.mainIngredient: Freshly prepared chicken (30%), freshly prepared salmon (14%), dried chicken (12%)
grainFreeDryFood.summary: Exceptional 80/20 formula from a small-batch UK manufacturer. Gently prepared at low temperatures to preserve nutrients. Six animal proteins in one recipe for a varied, biologically appropriate diet.
pros: 80% meat content — outstanding
Six different protein sources
Small-batch UK production
Low-temperature preparation preserves nutrients
cons: Not widely available in shops
Multiple proteins = not ideal for elimination diets
Price increase over last year

grainFreeDryFood.name: Harringtons Grain Free Turkey & Veg
grainFreeDryFood.summary: The most affordable grain-free option in the UK, widely available in supermarkets. Good entry point for dogs transitioning to grain-free. Lower meat content than premium options but genuine value for money.
pros: Incredible value at £2.00/kg
Available everywhere — Tesco, Asda, Pets at Home
No artificial colours, flavours, or preservatives
Good for transitioning to grain-free
cons: Only 30% meat — lowest on our list
Potato-heavy recipe
Basic nutritional profile

grainFreeDryFood.name: Forthglade Natural Lifestage Chicken
grainFreeDryFood.summary: Devon-based family brand making natural dog food since 1971. Grain-free cold-pressed option that's gentle on digestion. Known for excellent palatability and steady energy release.
pros: Cold-pressed for better digestion
Over 50 years of UK manufacturing
No synthetic preservatives
Steady energy release throughout the day
cons: Cold-pressed kibble different texture — some dogs take time to adjust
Moderate meat content
Smaller bag sizes available

grainFreeDryFood.name: Acana Classics Prairie Poultry
grainFreeDryFood.mainIngredient: Fresh chicken (12%), fresh turkey (4%), chicken meal (12%)
grainFreeDryFood.summary: From the same makers as Orijen but at a more accessible price point. 60% quality poultry ingredients with 40% fruit, vegetables, and botanicals. A solid mid-range option that delivers excellent nutrition.
pros: Same manufacturer as Orijen — trusted quality
Good balance of quality and price
Multiple poultry proteins
Includes wholesome fruits and botanicals
cons: Not as premium as Orijen sibling
Contains some legumes (lentils, chickpeas)
Can be harder to find in shops

freshDogFood.name: Years Fresh Steam-Cooked
freshDogFood.summary: Gently steam-cooked fresh food that's shelf-stable — no freezer or fridge needed until opened. Every recipe is grain- and legume-free, and it holds the highest-ever AADF rating (96%) for a whole-food meal. The cupboard-storage angle solves the biggest practical drawback of fresh feeding.
pros: Shelf-stable — no freezer space required (rare for fresh food)
Highest-ever AADF rating (96%) for a whole-food meal
All recipes grain- and legume-free — ideal for sensitive dogs
Recipes by veterinary clinical nutrition specialist + ex-luxury chef
Gentle cooking aids digestibility and palatability for fading appetites
cons: Pricing is personalised — must run the plan calculator for an exact figure
Newer brand with a shorter track record than rivals
No published affiliate programme yet (discount-code model)

freshDogFood.name: Butternut Box Fresh Cooked
freshDogFood.summary: The best-known UK fresh food brand — freshly cooked, frozen meals portioned precisely for your dog, with recipes that can be tailored around sensitivities. High moisture and strong palatability make it excellent for dogs who've gone off dry food, though it needs freezer space and is the priciest fresh option.
pros: Precisely portioned meals tailored to your dog's profile
Recipes customisable around allergies and sensitivities (wheat-free available)
Excellent palatability and high moisture — great for fussy or older dogs
Well-established, trusted UK brand with strong reviews
Generous ambassador/referral programme (£40/referral)
cons: Requires dedicated freezer space
Most expensive option — up to £6/day for large dogs
Frozen delivery logistics need planning

freshDogFood.name: Pure Pet Food Air-Dried
freshDogFood.summary: Air-dried food you rehydrate with warm water, giving many of the benefits of fresh food — digestibility, palatability, named ingredients — at a lower price and without freezer space. The rehydrated texture is soft, which suits seniors with dental wear or missing teeth. A sensible middle ground between kibble and full fresh.
pros: Cheapest fresh-style option — from £0.89/day for small dogs
Cupboard-stored, no freezer needed
Soft rehydrated texture suits seniors with dental issues
Grain-free recipes available, named whole ingredients
Lightweight and compact to store and post
cons: Not true fresh — air-dried then rehydrated
Requires preparation (adding water, waiting to rehydrate)
Texture won't suit every dog

freshDogFood.name: tails.com Tailored Blend
freshDogFood.summary: tails.com builds a kibble (and optional wet food) blend to your dog's profile — life stage, weight goals and sensitivities — and posts it through the door. Grain-free options are available. Cupboard-stored and convenient, with genuinely useful tailoring for seniors whose needs are shifting, though as a Nestlé Purina brand it's more mainstream than the boutique fresh options.
pros: Blend tailored to life stage, weight goal and sensitivities
Grain-free options available
Convenient letterbox delivery, cupboard-stored
Tailoring genuinely useful for changing senior needs
Easy to adjust the recipe as your dog ages
Awin affiliate programme (#6074) — straightforward to monetise
cons: Kibble, not true fresh food
Nestlé Purina-owned — more mainstream than boutique brands
Less of a step-up in quality than the fresh options

wetDogFood.name: Forthglade Complete Grain Free Wet
wetDogFood.summary: Devon-made grain-free wet food, gently steamed in trays with 75% single-source protein (salmon, lamb, duck, beef or turkey) and no fillers. Voted Best Wet Food by Your Dog Magazine readers six years running, and rated 77–82% by All About Dog Food depending on recipe (adult 77%, senior 82%, puppy 84%) — among the highest scores for any wet food. Hypoallergenic, all life stages, and gentle on sensitive tummies.
pros: 77–82% AADF rating (recipe-dependent) — among the highest for any UK wet food
75% single-source protein, ideal for elimination diets
Grain- and gluten-free, hypoallergenic across the range
Trays for puppy, adult and senior — works for multi-dog homes
British-made in Devon with no artificial additives
cons: Trays cost more per gram than supermarket tins
Single-protein recipes mean less flavour variety per pack
Best price needs a larger multi-tray subscription

wetDogFood.name: Nature's Menu Country Hunter Cans
wetDogFood.summary: Premium grain-free cans built around 80% real meat, with novel proteins like venison, duck and salmon plus superfood fruit and veg. The venison recipe scores a standout 88% on All About Dog Food. The novel-protein line-up makes it a strong pick for dogs reacting to common meats like chicken and beef.
pros: 80% real meat with superfood fruit and veg
Novel proteins (venison, duck, salmon) for sensitive dogs
Venison recipe rated 88% by AADF — class-leading
Grain-free and hypoallergenic, no artificial additives
cons: Higher cost per gram than mainstream cans
Rich novel-protein recipes can be too much for some dogs
Large 600g cans need fridge storage once opened

wetDogFood.name: Canagan Country Game Wet
wetDogFood.summary: From the well-regarded British grain-free brand, Canagan's wet cans pack 65% freshly prepared meat with sweet potato for slow-release energy, plus green-lipped mussel for joint support and prebiotics for digestion. The Country Game (venison, duck, goose) and Free-Run Chicken recipes mirror Canagan's popular kibble, making them an easy topper or standalone meal.
pros: 65% freshly prepared meat with named ingredients
Added green-lipped mussel (glucosamine) for joints
Prebiotics support digestion; sweet potato not cereal
Matches the Canagan kibble range for easy mixed feeding
100% recyclable packaging
cons: Premium price per can
Lower meat % than the very highest wet foods
Best availability online or in pet specialists, not supermarkets

wetDogFood.name: Lily's Kitchen Grain Free Wet
wetDogFood.summary: The recognisable British brand's grain-free tins use freshly prepared meat (60%+), vegetables and botanical herbs, steam-cooked with no meat meal, fillers or nasties. B Corp certified and widely stocked in supermarkets and pet shops, Lily's Kitchen is the most convenient way to buy genuinely natural wet food, with proper-food recipes most dogs lap up.
pros: 60%+ freshly prepared meat, no meat meal or fillers
B Corp certified — strong ethical credentials
Widely available in UK supermarkets and pet shops
Grain-free recipes with botanical herbs, well-liked by fussy dogs
cons: Pricier per kg than specialist trays for the meat content
AADF scores trail the very top wet foods
Recipe tweaks over the years have divided some long-term buyers

wetDogFood.name: Wainwright's Grain Free Wet
wetDogFood.summary: Pets at Home's own-brand wet food is the value pick: single-source protein (turkey, duck or lamb), hypoallergenic with no added wheat, pork, dairy, soya or artificial additives, and made in the UK. At around £1.80 a 395g can it undercuts the boutique brands while still being grain-free and easy to digest — a sensible everyday choice on a budget.
pros: Cheapest grain-free wet on our list — around £1.80/can
Single-source protein, hypoallergenic recipe
No added wheat, pork, dairy, soya or artificial additives
Easy to buy in-store and online at Pets at Home
UK-made with Easy Repeat discounts available
cons: Lower meat content than premium brands
Own-brand, so less ingredient transparency than boutiques
Only sold through Pets at Home

puppyFood.name: Forthglade Cold Pressed Puppy (Turkey)
pros: Cold-pressed dry rated 73–75% by AADF and wet 77–88% — among the highest puppy scores in the UK
Genuinely all-life-stage: the same recipe works from 8 weeks to senior, so no stressful diet switch as your pup grows
Grain-, gluten-free and hypoallergenic across the range — gentle on developing digestion
Turkey Cold Pressed Puppies & Small Dogs uses a smaller pellet ideal for tiny mouths and teething pups
Affordable Puppy Pack lets you trial dry, wet and treats before committing
cons: Cold-pressed kibble is denser than extruded — soak for very young pups
Premium versus supermarket puppy kibble
Best per-unit price needs a larger subscription
puppyFood.summary: Our top all-round puppy pick. Forthglade's cold-pressed dry food (73–75% AADF) and wet trays (77–88% AADF) are among the highest-rated puppy options in the UK, and uniquely they're all-life-stage — the same grain-free recipe carries from weaning right through to senior, sparing you the diet switches most brands force. A smaller-pellet Turkey recipe suits small breeds and teething mouths, and the low-cost Puppy Pack is the easiest way to trial it.

puppyFood.name: Butternut Box Fresh Puppy
bestFor: Owners wanting fresh from day one
pros: Gently steam-cooked fresh food rated 93–94% by AADF — top-tier nutrition for growth
60% fresh meat with no grains, soya, sugar or salt — easy on developing tummies and skin
Portions calculated to your puppy's current weight and growth target, recalculated as they grow
Pre-portioned pouches make controlled growth simple — overfeeding large-breed pups risks joint problems
Two-week intro plan to check your pup takes to it before a bigger commitment
cons: Premium pricing — roughly £2.40/day for a small growing pup, more for larger breeds
Needs fridge and freezer space
Subscription model rather than buy-as-you-go
puppyFood.summary: The fresh-food choice for puppies. Butternut Box's steam-cooked meals score 93–94% on AADF, use 60% fresh meat and skip grains, soya, sugar and salt — excellent for growth and gentle on sensitive young tummies and skin. The killer feature for puppies is portion control: meals are calculated to your pup's current weight and growth target and recalculated as they grow, which matters because overfeeding a large-breed puppy can cause lasting joint problems. From around £2.40/day for a small pup, with a two-week intro plan.

puppyFood.name: Pooch & Mutt Puppy Complete Chicken & Superfood
pros: Cheapest grain-free premium puppy dry food we rate — strong value at 66–77% AADF
Lean chicken plus pumpkin and salmon oil for fatty acids, antioxidants and a healthy coat
Simple one-recipe range — no decision paralysis
Widely stocked and easy to buy in bulk (10kg bag) to bring the per-day cost down
cons: AADF score sits below Forthglade and the fresh brands
Single chicken recipe — no option if your pup reacts to poultry
Less ingredient variety than premium fresh meals
puppyFood.summary: The best-value pick. Pooch & Mutt's grain-free Puppy Complete Chicken & Superfood is the cheapest premium grain-free puppy kibble we'd recommend, yet still rates 66–77% on AADF. Lean chicken is boosted with pumpkin and salmon oil for omega fatty acids, antioxidants and coat health. A single, no-fuss recipe that buys well in bulk — the sensible choice if you want quality grain-free nutrition without fresh-food prices.

puppyFood.name: Years Fresh Puppy
pros: 95% AADF — among the highest-rated puppy meals available anywhere
Shelf-stable: fresh nutrition without needing freezer space (rare for fresh food)
Grain- and legume-free across the range — relevant given grain-free DCM concerns hinge on legumes/pulses, not grain alone
Recipes designed by a veterinary clinical nutrition specialist
Low-cost trial to test before committing
cons: Pricing is personalised — run the plan calculator for an exact figure
Newer brand with a shorter track record
Discount-code model, no published affiliate programme yet
puppyFood.summary: The nutrition leader. Years' steam-cooked fresh meals hold a 95% AADF rating — about as high as puppy food gets — yet are shelf-stable, so you get fresh-grade nutrition without surrendering freezer space. Every recipe is grain- AND legume-free (worth noting, since the grain-free heart-health concern centres on legumes and pulses rather than grain itself), and recipes are formulated by a veterinary clinical nutrition specialist. A low-cost trial makes it easy to test.

puppyFood.name: Pure Pet Food Puppy
pros: Air-dried whole ingredients rehydrated at home — fresh-style quality that stores in the cupboard
Lightweight and long-life — easy for travel and small homes
Personalised to your puppy's weight and growth, like the fresh subscriptions
Rehydrated texture is soft and tempting for teething or fussy pups
cons: AADF range (61–74%) is solid rather than class-leading
Needs preparing (add warm water and wait) before each meal
Costs more than dry kibble per day
puppyFood.summary: The fresh-without-the-freezer alternative. Pure Pet Food air-dries whole ingredients that you rehydrate at home, giving fresh-style quality (61–74% AADF) that stores in the cupboard and travels well. Like the fresh subscriptions it's personalised to your puppy's weight and growth, and the soft rehydrated texture suits teething and fussy pups. From around £0.89/day — a middle ground between kibble and chilled fresh.
